name: CheckSmart
description_short: At 2196 Brookpark Rd, Cleveland, OH 44134, CheckSmart can be reached by calling 1-216-351-3366.
description_long:
CheckSmart is a regional financial services retailer operating a network of brick-and-mortar stores across Ohio, with a strong presence in the Columbus metropolitan area. The company serves consumers who need fast access to financial services outside the traditional banking system — including the unbanked, underbanked, and those with time-sensitive needs. With five Columbus locations alone (Morse Rd, Dublin Granville Rd, Sawmill Rd, Wilson Rd, Westpointe Plaza Dr) plus stores in Heath, Marion, Marysville, Reynoldsburg, Springfield, and Whitehall, CheckSmart offers meaningful geographic reach across central Ohio.

The company's core in-store financial services include check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and bill payment. For consumers needing short-term credit, CheckSmart facilitates access to loan products from an unaffiliated third-party lender — it does not underwrite or fund loans directly in Ohio. The loan application is entirely in-person and requires a government-issued ID, proof of employment or income, a working phone number, a valid email address, and proof of address. CheckSmart locations also issue Green Dot Visa Debit Cards and allow customers to exchange unused gift cards for cash, rounding out a broad financial menu under one roof.

CheckSmart differentiates itself through extended hours and a multi-service convenience model. Friday hours stretch from 8AM to 8PM, wider than many competing storefronts, and Sunday service (11AM–5PM) is available — uncommon in the check cashing sector. Online appointment scheduling is offered to reduce in-store wait times for loan applicants, and staff walk customers through the application from arrival to funding decision.

For Ohio residents without bank accounts or those needing immediate financial services, CheckSmart fills a genuine gap — particularly for check cashing, money orders, and wire transfers. For short-term loans, however, the picture is more complex: because CheckSmart is not the direct lender, loan rates, fees, and APR are not disclosed on the store page, making cost comparison impossible before an in-person visit. Short-term loans facilitated through storefront intermediaries typically carry high APRs, and the absence of any public rate disclosure is a meaningful caveat. Borrowers should ask explicitly about total repayment cost, origination fees, and the lender's identity before proceeding.
pros: Five Columbus-area locations plus 11 additional Ohio stores for broad local access
Extended Friday hours (8AM–8PM) and Sunday availability (11AM–5PM), uncommon for check cashers
Online appointment scheduling available to speed up in-store loan process
Green Dot Visa Debit Card issued in-store — practical for unbanked customers
Multiple financial services under one roof — no need to visit separate providers
Staff-assisted loan application with fast in-store lending decisions
cons: CheckSmart is not the direct lender in Ohio — loans are underwritten by an unnamed third-party, adding opacity to terms and complaints process
No loan rates, APR, or fee information disclosed on the website — borrowers cannot comparison-shop before visiting
Loan applications require an in-person store visit; no fully online process available
Reduced Saturday hours (9AM–4PM) and Sunday hours (11AM–5PM) limit weekend access
Third-party lending model typical of this sector is associated with high-cost short-term credit
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked Ohio residents who need check cashing without a traditional bank account
Consumers needing money orders, wire transfers, or bill payment services in person
Individuals who want to exchange unused gift cards for cash
Borrowers with urgent short-term credit needs who prefer face-to-face service over online lenders
diagnosis: CheckSmart is best suited for Ohio residents who need immediate check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, or bill payment services and prefer handling finances in person at a neighborhood storefront. The main caveat is that their loan products are facilitated through an unnamed third-party lender with no public rate disclosure, making this a poor choice for cost-conscious borrowers who can qualify elsewhere — but a viable last-resort option for those with limited alternatives.
services: Green Dot Visa Debit Card issuance
